name: SOC Detection Lab — Technique Coverage
description: Techniques exercised across IR-001 through IR-009 in the SOC Detection Lab portfolio project. Color reflects Wazuh detection status, not severity. Import this file directly at https://mitre-attack.github.io/attack-navigator/

techniques.comment: IR-001 — confirmed detected via Wazuh stock rules 60122/60204 on a burst of 21x Event ID 4625.
IR-002 — confirmed detected via Wazuh stock rule 92057 on a Sysmon Event ID 1 commandLine containing -EncodedCommand.
IR-003 — confirmed detected via Wazuh stock rule 60154 on Event ID 4732 (Administrators group changed).
IR-004 — Defender Operational log channel wasn't forwarded at all; fixed via ossec.conf. Whether a rule fires on Event 5007 now that it's forwarded has not been retested.
IR-005 — Wazuh is a HIDS and never sees an external Nmap scan; no Windows Event Log entry is generated. Out of scope for this stack without a NIDS or firewall-log forwarding.

IR-006 — confirmed detected via Wazuh stock rule 63103 on Event ID 1102. Originally misdocumented as a gap due to a flawed search; corrected after the alert was found firing correctly, including on the original incident date.

IR-007 — Event 4698 (now covered by stock rule 60228) and Event 106 logging gaps both fixed at the OS audit-policy/log-channel level. A custom Wazuh rule (100004) targeting the same technique via Sysmon does not fire; root cause unresolved.
IR-008 — static triage of a sourced phishing sample. Analysis exercise only; not a live SIEM detection in this lab (no mail gateway or SIEM was in the path).

IR-009 — hash-based OSINT triage of a real Gh0stRAT/SilverFox/ValleyRAT sample sourced from MalwareBazaar. Analysis exercise only; sample was never executed, so this is not a live detection in this lab.
IR-009 supporting technique — anti-VM/anti-sandbox YARA match observed in the sample's static analysis only. Never executed or tested live.
IR-009 supporting technique — C2 domain/IP documented from MalwareBazaar IOC data only. Never contacted or tested live.

legendItems.label: Confirmed detected — stock or custom Wazuh rule fires
Partial — logging gap fixed, rule coverage open/unresolved
No detection — out of scope for this HIDS-only stack
Analysis/OSINT exercise — not a live SIEM detection in this lab
